title: zea perennis subsp. diploperennis
intro: Zea perennis subsp. diploperennis, also known as the wild or diploid teosinte, is a subspecies of the wild ancestor of maize. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Zea perennis subsp. diploperennis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Zea perennis subsp. diploperennis can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
